brief_title: Survey Study: Financial Impact of Breast Cancer Treatment
official_title: Determining the Financial Impact of Breast Cancer Treatment Over a One Year Period for Patients at a Rural Cancer Center
brief_summary: A survey study to evaluate the financial impact of breast cancer as self-reported by breast cancer patients over a period of 1 year. The study will also look at the health insurance literacy and patient's utilization of support services.

interventions.name: Quality of Life Surveys
interventions.description: Patients with breast cancer diagnosis complete questionnaires over 20-30 minutes at baseline and at 3, 6, and 12 months after enrollment.

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* 18 years or older
* Diagnosed with breast cancer or a recurrence of breast cancer within the past 2 years
* Willing to sign consent
* Able to read and complete surveys in English

Exclusion Criteria:

* Cancer diagnosis other than breast cancer
* More than 2 years since diagnosis with breast cancer or recurrence

primary_outcomes.measure: Change in level of self-reported financial burden
primary_outcomes.description: Will evaluate the change in Comprehensive Score for Financial Toxicity (COST-FACIT) measurements from baseline to 1 year follow up. This survey consists of 14 questions related to finances.Responses include 5 options with lower scores representing worse outcomes. Scores at 1 year will be compared to baseline scores.
